text.title: Cloud Run Vpc Access Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the cloud-run-vpc-access methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Egress to private CIDRs goes through public internet (or fails).
antipattern.root-cause: `--vpc-egress=all-traffic` not set OR no connector configured.
antipattern.fix: Configure connector OR direct VPC egress; set egress to `private-ranges-only` when no internet needed.

antipattern.symptom: Tail latency spikes; throttled egress under load.
antipattern.root-cause: Connector autoscaler at max instances for >1h during peak.
antipattern.fix: Resize connector min/max instances; consider direct VPC egress (gen2) instead.

antipattern.symptom: Internal-only service is reachable from public internet; IAM is the only barrier.
IP space exhaustion; quota issues on connector count.
antipattern.root-cause: Each service has its own connector pointing to the same VPC.
antipattern.fix: Consolidate to one shared connector per region per VPC.
